# terrible_purestorage_flasharray_purefa_offload (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the offload target

### Optional

- `access_key` (String) Access Key ID of the offload target
- `account` (String) Name of the Azure blob storage account
- `address` (String) The IP or FQDN address of the NFS server
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auth_region` (String) The region that will be used for initial authentication request. This parameter is optional and should be used only when region autodetection fails.
- `bucket` (String) Name of the bucket for the S3 or GCP target
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `container` (String) Name of the blob container of the Azure target
- `context` (String) Name of fleet member on which to perform the operation. This requires the array receiving the request is a member of a fleet and the context name to be a member of the same fleet.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `initialize` (Boolean) Define whether to initialize the offload bucket
- `options` (String) Additonal mount options for the NFS share Supported mount options include *port*, *rsize*, *wsize*, *nfsvers*, and *tcp* or *udp*
- `placement` (String) AWS S3 placement strategy
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `profile` (String) The Offload target profile that will be selected for this target. This option allows more granular configuration for the target on top of the protocol parameter
- `protocol` (String) Define which protocol the offload engine uses NFS is not a supported protocl from Purity//FA 6.6.0 and higher
- `secret` (String) Secret Access Key for the offload target
- `share` (String) NFS export on the NFS server
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Define state of offload
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `uri` (String) The URI used to create a connection between the array and a non-AWS S3 offload target. Storage placement strategies are not supported for non-AWS S3 offload targets. Both the HTTP and HTTPS protocols are allowed.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
